source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the figure on the right a few of the small squares will be painted grey. In so doing no square <image1> that is made from four small grey squares must appear. At most how many of the squares in the figure can be painted grey?
<image2>

image_dependency: question explicitly says "In the figure" and does not describe the full layout in text
image_manifest shows 1 existing image with registry_passed=true
multi_step: task asks for an optimum ("At most how many") rather than direct lookup
constraint must be enforced globally: "no square made from four small grey squares may appear"
solving likely requires combining figure layout from the image with counting/search or case analysis
verifiability: answer_text is a single clear value: "21"
constraint is explicit, so a candidate coloring can in principle be checked against the rule
asset registry passed and both question and answer are present

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and required image are all present, so the core semantics are intact.
Asset registry passed with no recorded issues, indicating the sample is complete enough for candidacy.
Initial scores are strong: high image dependency, good multi-step potential, and high verifiability.
Although the problem depends on the figure and involves global optimization, those are task characteristics rather than asset-loss issues.
